untranslatable_reason: This signal requires specialized options pricing analysis and arbitrage relationship detection (conversions/reversals, vertical spreads, butterflies, straddles, time spreads with mispricing magnitude evaluation). The allowed indicator set contains only equity price/volume/technical indicators and does not include: (1) options pricing metrics (implied volatility, Greeks, option bid-ask spreads), (2) option relationship pricing comparisons (conversion/reversal parity checks), (3) relative mispricing quantification between option legs, or (4) strategy-specific arbitrage conditions. Implementing this rule requires real-time options market data, multi-leg relationship pricing logic, and volatility surface analysis — none of which are available in the provided indicator library. This is fundamentally an options-specific trading strategy that cannot be translated to equity technical indicators.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_111_0 is from 'Option Volatility and Pricing' by Sheldon Natenberg and describes systematic option arbitrage exploitation. While the signal structure implies entry/exit conditions and regime preferences, the core logic (identifying mispricings in option relationships and comparing strategy complexity levels) cannot be expressed using only equity price-action and technical indicators. A proper backtest would require an options pricing engine with Greek calculations and implied volatility surfaces.
